title: Keep post-hold board truth aligned with rolling short-window congestion
summary: Patched the distribution lane selector so once the board's earlier post-hold release time passes, a newly extended rolling short review window triggers a fresh distribution-architecture repair instead of resurfacing the same primary-repo-flat packet or drifting into owned-content fallback.
shared_findings_used: marketing_execution_board_latest.md: board said the current primary-repo-flat packet becomes eligible after 2026-05-26T20:55:18
distribution_lane_latest.md: no truthful do-now packet exists during the active short review window
adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg remains flat, so fake do-now resurfacing would waste the slot
marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: primary_repo_flat repair is still measurement-pending and should prefer truthful architecture repair over stale fallback
evidence: At 2026-05-26T20:56:00 the selector was resurfacing primary_repo_flat_contact_handoff_packet even though newer live actions had extended short-window congestion until 2026-05-26T22:47:35.
After blocking that stale resurfacing, the selector could drift to owned_content fallback instead of acknowledging the board truth had become stale and needed repair.
expected_outcome: When the current short review window is extended by newer live actions after an earlier board release timestamp, the next rerun refreshes execution-board truth instead of surfacing a stale packet or falling back to owned content.
